old_claim.note: Animal Husbandry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The plant extract is applied within a sequence of mechanical and chemical skin-processing operations that converts raw hide into leather.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and Introduction define leather production as mechanical and chemical treatment of raw skin and identify an aqueous plant extract used for tanning.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Schnidejoch claim is a specific chemical stage within hide-to-leather processing, evidenced by plant biomarkers in worked leather artifacts.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Generic animal management is not necessary because hides can come from wild animals, while prior skin processing is intrinsic to the documented tanning operation.

ontology_before: Plant tanning was tied to animal husbandry even though domestic stock is not a universal source of hides.
ontology_after: Plant tanning is modeled as a required stage within the broader leather-working process.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: leather_tanning now requires leather_working and no longer depends on animal_husbandry.
would_reject_if: The Schnidejoch material were shown to be untreated raw hide rather than worked and tanned leather.
short_reason: Tanning is part of leather working; husbandry is only one possible hide source.
